metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A colorful ornamental avian folk city where Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans, Patchwork Hall Beacon, Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Auric Plume should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Auric Plume organizes power through Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel, public response through Patchwork Hall Beacon, civic memory through Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans, and daily life through S1 S1 Patchwork Hall, S2 Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 S5 Harbor Campus.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Seashell, Coral Stone, Pale Marble Stone, Canvas, Silk, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight, and exact anchors including Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel, Patchwork Hall Beacon, Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans, Harbor Basilica, Cargo Yard, Star Observatory, S1 Patchwork Hall, S1 Patchwork Pillar, S1 Patchwork Kiosk, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A colorful ornamental avian folk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans, Patchwork Hall Beacon, Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel, and section anchors such as S1 Patchwork Hall, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Public Works Authority Hall, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Harbor Campus.

canonLocks: Auric Plume uses city code APE.
The Wonder is Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans.
The Beacon is Patchwork Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.
The source-backed section spine is: S1 S1 Patchwork Hall, S2 Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 S5 Harbor Campus.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Auric Plume as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Auric Plume's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

forms: Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans
skyline: Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans
infrastructure: records, permits, markets, services, or rituals implied by anchors
colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as colorful ornamental avian folk and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as Harbor Basilica.
customs: citizens orient public life around S1 Patchwork Hall in Section 1
citizens orient public life around Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall in Section 2
citizens orient public life around Public Works Authority Hall in Section 3
citizens orient public life around Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall in Section 4
citizens orient public life around S5 Harbor Campus in Section 5
beacon changes become public behavior, not background color
exact landmark names carry social memory and directions
civic errands happen visibly at thresholds, queues, ledgers, stalls, courts, or shrines
bodyLanguage: workers move with practiced familiarity around district anchors
officials use thresholds and raised places to control attention
residents treat named landmarks as everyday orientation points
speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors
local speech distinguishes civic authority, ordinary errands, and landmark obligations
people refer to D13 as the seat of power rather than a faction headquarters

streets.appearance: Streets should show Auric Plume's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 S1 Patchwork Hall, S2 Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, S3 Public Works Authority Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 S5 Harbor Campus. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
